title: UX Widget IncludeScript
text: UX Widget IncludeScript UX widgets (field, entity, and component) can define IncludeScripts to include fragments of ProcScript and JavaScript, which can be used to define the widget-specific code and definitions in one place. This can be done by specifying properties in the physical widget definition section of the UX widget, which refer to the IncludeScript to be included in the script or declarations container of the widget. The Uniface compiler inserts the IncludeScript into the DSP component using #include directives during the compilation process. For more information, see IncludeScript . Related Links #include Insert the contents of the specified IncludeScript in the location of the #include directive. How to define a UX Widget IncludeScript UX widgets (field, entity, and component) can specify IncludeScript for their script and declarations containers in the physical definition of the widget. Inheritance Order of UX Widget IncludeScript For a UX Widget that includes the IncludeScript widget property definition, the corresponding IncludeScript fragments are added to the component by the compiler. If multiple definitions of ProcScript modules and #define declarations are present, the Uniface compiler follows the inheritance precedence order. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
